items.headline: Wall Street Continues Upward Trend Amidst Record-Low Consumer Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. equities extended their rally today, marking an impressive eighth consecutive winning week for the S&P 500, with all major indices closing higher. This robust market performance, however, contrasts sharply with a University of Michigan survey revealing consumer sentiment has fallen to a record low, primarily driven by persistent inflation concerns and high oil prices due to the ongoing conflict with Iran. AI-driven growth continues to be a significant market driver, alongside a notable resurgence in the healthcare sector.

items.headline: Ross Stores Rises on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Ross Stores reported profit and revenue that comfortably beat analysts' expectations, leading to a significant rally.

items.headline: Estee Lauder Jumps on Merger News
items.summary: The beauty company saw its shares climb after announcing it was no longer considering a potential merger with Puig.

items.headline: Workday Exceeds Profit Expectations
items.summary: Workday's stock rose following a better-than-anticipated profit report for the latest quarter.

items.headline: Zoom Communications Posts Strong Profit
items.summary: Zoom's shares advanced after the company delivered better profit results than analysts had expected.

items.headline: Nvidia Shares Decline
items.summary: Despite strong earnings, Nvidia's stock experienced a modest pullback today.

items.headline: Walmart Sees Modest Dip
items.summary: Walmart's stock edged lower during Friday's trading session.

items.headline: Amazon Shares Close Down
items.summary: Amazon's stock concluded the day with a slight decrease.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low Amid Inflation Fears
items.summary: A University of Michigan survey indicates U.S. consumer sentiment has plummeted to its lowest point in history, falling below the 2022 inflation peak. Households express deep concern over rising inflation, with forecasts predicting a worsening to 4.8% in the next 12 months. Gasoline prices exceeding $4.50 per gallon are a significant contributor to this sentiment.

items.headline: Fed Rate Cut Bets Vanish as Inflation Worries Persist
items.summary: Traders on Wall Street have removed expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts later this year, signaling heightened concerns about persistent inflation.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Strong as 100% of S&P 500 Firms Beat EPS Estimates
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season is concluding on a high note, with all 19 S&P 500 companies that reported this week exceeding EPS expectations. Notably, 79% of these firms also achieved year-over-year earnings growth.

items.headline: Nvidia Crushes Expectations with $81.62 Billion Revenue
items.summary: Nvidia ($NVDA) delivered stellar Q1 FY2027 results, reporting revenue of $81.62 billion, an 85% year-over-year increase, and significantly surpassing consensus estimates. The company also issued an optimistic Q2 guidance of $91 billion, further solidifying the AI infrastructure supercycle thesis.

items.headline: Tech and Healthcare Lead, Utilities Lag in Sector Performance
items.summary: Technology stocks are showing a significant rebound, with the State Street Tech Select Sector SPDR ETF ($XLK) up 10.6% in May and 22.3% year-to-date. Healthcare also had its best week in six months, surging over 3%. Conversely, the utilities sector (State Street Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF, $XLU) has been the biggest loser in May, sliding 4.9% amidst concerns about the impact of AI data centers on energy and water supplies.

items.headline: War with Iran Continues to Drive Oil Prices and Inflation
items.summary: The ongoing conflict with Iran continues to exert upward pressure on oil prices, contributing to elevated inflation and negatively impacting consumer sentiment.

items.headline: Wall Street Braces for Potential IPO Wave
items.summary: Following Cerebras' successful semiconductor debut, Wall Street is anticipating an influx of high-profile IPOs. Companies like SpaceX and OpenAI are reportedly preparing for potential listings, indicating a readiness for new public offerings.
